revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:会符]]
'''Efu''' (会符) refers to a shipping tag used during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} by the {{ill|Imperial Court of Japan|ja|朝廷 (日本)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Imperial Court of Japan|qid=Q30253177}} (Imperial Court), the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (shogunate), {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|qid=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} (court nobles), {{ill|buke|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/buke|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (samurai families), and {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}} to indicate the ownership of transported goods. It was also known as '''Kōrifu''' (行李符), '''Efu''' (絵符), or '''Denfu''' (伝符). == Description == For the Imperial Court and the shogunate, the tags were marked simply with "{{ill|Goyō|ja|御用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御用達|1=Royal warrant of appointment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1070771}}" alongside the {{ill|Chrysanthemum Seal|ja|菊花紋章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chrysanthemum Seal|1=Kikumon (chrysanthemum crests)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11082563}} or {{ill|Triple Hollyhock|ja|三つ葉葵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triple Hollyhock|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葵紋|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353976}} crests. In other cases, the tags would indicate the specific affiliation, such as "○○ Goyō" or "×× Household ÷÷ Goyō," accompanied by family crests. Goods bearing a Efu tag were granted special privileges, allowing transportation at the government-regulated '''{{ill|Osadame Chinsen|ja|御定賃銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osadame Chinsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' rates when utilizing {{ill|Shukuba|en|Shukuba|qid=Q1209783|lt=post stations|1=Shukuba}} and {{ill|relay horses|ja|伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/relay horses|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 駅伝制|1=post-horses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381453}}. Notably, goods designated for the {{ill|Tokugawa family|ja|徳川将軍家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳川宗家|1=Tokugawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071640}} (shōgun's household goods) were mandated to be transported free of charge. == Issues in the 18th Century == From the mid-18th century, as commercial distribution flourished, the disparity between the government-regulated Osadame Chinsen and the market-based '''{{ill|Aitai Chinsen|ja|相対賃銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aitai Chinsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' grew. Consequently, nobles, samurai, and temples began leasing Efu tags to {{ill|merchants|ja|Merchant|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/merchants|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Baixing|en|Baixing|qid=Q843939|lt=farmers|1=Baixing}} for a fee, enabling them to benefit from the lower Osadame Chinsen rates. Although the shogunate frequently prohibited this practice, such bans proved ineffective. == References == * Yukitaka Kodama, "Efu" in ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten 2|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten 2|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1980), ISBN 978-4-642-00502-9 * Yasunari Maruyama, "Efu" in ''{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten 1|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (Heibonsha, 1992), ISBN 978-4-582-13101-7
== See Also == * {{ill|Edo Period Transportation|ja|日本の交通史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Period Transportation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japanese Feudal System|ja|日本の封建制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Feudal System|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|会符|version=97910739|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:会符]]
